samples.gvd:
module RAM
#(
    parameter WIDTH = 6,
    parameter DEPTH = 8
)
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] read_addr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0] read_data
);

reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        for (int i = 0; i < DEPTH; i++) begin
            mem[i] <= 6'b000000;
        end
    end
    else if (write_en) begin
        mem[write_addr] <= write_data;
    end
end

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        read_data <= 6'b000000;
    end
    else if (read_en) begin
        read_data <= mem[read_addr];
    end
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. 6 8 Loop variable for the for loop.

Define a 6-bit wide and 8-depth RAM array using the reg data type. Initialize all the memory locations to 000000.

reg [DEPTH - 1 : 0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0] = '{2**WIDTH{6'b000000}};

Use an always block that is triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin

Check if the reset signal is active. If it is, use a for loop to iterate through all the memory locations of the RAM array.

if (!rst_n) begin for (i = 0; i < 2**WIDTH; i = i + 1) begin

Set each memory location to 000000.

RAM[i] <= 6'b000000; end

Check if the write enable signal is active. If it is, use the write_addr input to select the memory location and write the write_data input to that location.

else if (write_en) begin RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;

Check if the read enable signal is active. If it is, use the read_addr input to select the memory location and read that location into the read_data output.

if (read_en) begin read_data <= RAM[read_addr];

Check if the reset signal is active. If it is, set the read_data output to 000000.

else begin read_data <= 6'b000000; end end

Use an always block that is triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin

Check if the reset signal is active. If it is, set the read_data output to 000000.

if (!rst_n) begin read_data <= 6'b000000;

Check if the read enable signal is active. If it is, use the read_addr input to select the memory location and read that location into the read_data output.

else if (read_en) begin read_data <= RAM[read_addr];

Check if the write enable signal is active. If it is, use the write_addr input to select the memory location and write the write_data input to that location.

else if (write_en) begin RAM[write_addr] <= write_data; end end
